task_completion.description: Garmin BaseCamp application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of Garmin BaseCamp is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The import file dialog is opened in BaseCamp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer dialog (e.g., 'Import into My Collection') is open over the BaseCamp application.

task_completion.description: The correct GPX file is selected from the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'dole_langres_track.gpx' is highlighted or entered in the file name field of the import dialog, and the current directory is shown as the Desktop.

task_completion.description: The GPX track appears in the BaseCamp library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The track name (e.g., 'dole_langres_track' or similar route name) is visible in the 'My Collection' or user data pane on the left side of the BaseCamp interface.

task_completion.description: The imported GPS track is rendered on the map.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible route/track line is drawn on the main map area in BaseCamp, representing the path.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to import the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing command-line file manipulation or database insertion to bypass the BaseCamp UI.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with Garmin BaseCamp.
integrity.visual_evidence: The active application is explicitly Garmin BaseCamp, not a text editor, web browser, or a different GIS application like QGIS.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply open the GPX file as text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GPX XML code is not visible in a text editor; it must be processed and displayed as a map track by BaseCamp.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The imported file 'dole_langres_track.gpx' contains a synthetic GPS track from Dole, France to Champigny-lès-Langres, France. The route distance is approximately 124 km. If the agent opens the track properties in BaseCamp, these locations or the ~124 km distance should be visible.
